---
title: "Interactive Base R Tutorial"
# Purpose: Introduce the tutorial and provide the first browser-based R example.
---

{{< include ./_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}

<!-- Purpose: This page gives beginners the scope of the tutorial and a first editable webR cell. -->

This site is a small, browser-based introduction to Base R for ecology and plant
science. Each page uses interactive R cells that run in your browser with webR,
so you can edit and run code without installing R first.

The first lessons focus on the pieces used in many simple analyses:

- vectors for storing measurements
- data frames for rectangular data
- base plots for quick visual checks
- short exercises for practice

## Try R in the browser

Click **Run Code**, then change one of the plant heights and run it again.

```{webr}
#| autorun: true
height_cm <- c(12.1, 14.5, 13.8, 16.2, 15.4)

mean(height_cm)
range(height_cm)
```

## Small tutorial dataset

The site includes a small CSV file at `data/plant_growth.csv`. Later pages use
it to practice reading data, summarizing plant growth, and making quick Base R
plots.
